Controls the degree of referent removal during generation. S1 anonymized (default) and S2 category-level are implemented; S3 invented lexicon is deferred.

Severity sets how aggressively generation and filtering remove referents. S1 forbids proper nouns, numbers, dates, and named real entities while allowing generic common nouns. S2 additionally prefers category-level terms over specific kinds. S3, a global invented lexicon, is a separate deferred node.

Severity is the dial on the degree of knowledge absence. It simultaneously shapes the generation system prompt and the strictness of the leakage filter. S1 keeps generic nouns such as forest, lake, animal, stone. S2 pushes toward generic categories such as a creature, a substance, a structure, a body of water. The most extreme rung, a persistent invented content-word vocabulary, is tracked separately and not implemented in the first pass.
